decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). Per optimization history, geometry changes are hitting legality limits and yielding diminishing returns. The strategy shifts to material tuning as the primary lever. We will increase the heat spreader conductivity by the maximum allowed factor (2.0x) to enhance lateral heat spreading and further minimize the chip temperature.

changes.reason: Maximize lateral heat spreading capability within the allowed 2.0x step ratio to reduce peak chip temperature, as geometry adjustments have stalled.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than spreader lateral resistance
Material value of 1000 W/(m*K) is high but physically plausible for advanced composites or theoretical limits in this teaching case
